title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Poirot, Spence, and Elspeth discuss past murder cases to identify potential connections to the recent death of Joyce Reynolds. They first examine the case of Leslie Firrell, a law firm clerk at Fullerton, Harrison & Ladbate who was stabbed from behind near the Green Swan Inn eighteen months prior. Firrell had a criminal record for forgery and possessed significant unexplained cash, making the innkeeper Harry Griffin, his wife, or members of Firrell's gang potential suspects. Poirot dismisses this case as unlikely to be related to Joyce.

The discussion then turns to the death of Janet White, a teacher who was strangled on a shortcut two and a half years ago. Janet's roommate, Nora Ambrose, had noted that Janet was receiving threatening letters from an unnamed man following a breakup. Poirot finds this case more relevant, theorizing that Joyce may have witnessed a murder in the past—possibly Janet's—and only realized the significance of the event as she grew older. He suggests Joyce was killed because she claimed to have seen a murder while preparing for a Halloween party.

Spence provides a list of eighteen people present during the party preparations, including Mrs. Dreyfus, Miss Whitaker, Reverend Charles Cotterell, and the Reynolds family, though he notes the list may be incomplete due to people arriving and leaving. Finally, Poirot meets with Dr. Ferguson, a Scottish doctor, who discusses the cruelty of the child's death and the dangers posed by mentally deranged individuals in society.
